methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $146M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $500M - $32.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series B), post-money $5.6B

data.narrative:
Ambient.ai is a Series B-stage AI & Software company that has raised $146M to date. Valuation analysis relies on two primary methods triangulated against a peer set of 5 same-sector, same-stage comparables: Safe Superintelligence ($32.0B), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B), World Labs ($5.0B), Nominal ($1.0B), and Photoroom ($500M). The broader comp universe spans 9 private companies and 1 public reference point (Palantir).

Stage-progression methodology applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $277M from the $146M raised-proxy baseline. Peer-median triangulation across the five same-stage AI & Software comparables produces a $5.0B implied valuation, though the peer set exhibits substantial dispersion (range: $500M–$32.0B), reflecting wide heterogeneity in perceived technical maturity and market opportunity within frontier AI. 

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated valuation range of $236M (low) to $5.8B (high), with a median estimate of $2.6B. The wide dispersion between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects structural tension: stage-progression is conservative relative to peer benchmarks, likely because the raised-to-date proxy underweights Ambient.ai's operational progress or market positioning. Key uncertainties include revenue traction, product differentiation within a crowded frontier-AI landscape, and whether peer valuations in this cohort reflect durable fundamentals or momentum-driven pricing.
